per_query.question: What is the GhostVLAD approach?

per_query.oc_prediction: The GhostVLAD approach is a pooling strategy that enhances NetVLAD by introducing **G ghost clusters** (typically 2–4) to absorb noisy or irrelevant features during aggregation. It uses soft assignment clustering (like NetVLAD) but adds ghost clusters to explicitly model and exclude noise, improving robustness in utterance-level representation. Applied to ResNet-extracted spectrogram features, it computes compact, discriminative embeddings for language classification via a projection layer and softmax, achieving state-of-the-art performance (98.43% F1-score) in multilingual, real-world audio environments.
per_query.bl_prediction: The GhostVLAD approach is an extension of the NetVLAD pooling method, originally proposed for face recognition. It enhances NetVLAD by introducing additional "ghost clusters" alongside the standard clusters. These ghost clusters (typically 2-4) capture noisy or irrelevant features during the aggregation of frame-level features into utterance-level representations. While the NetVLAD layer computes a matrix $ V $ using both normal clusters ($ K $) and ghost clusters ($ G $), the ghost clusters are excluded during feature concatenation. This mechanism reduces noise impact by allowing ghost clusters to absorb irrelevant information, while normal clusters retain discriminative language features. The method improves language identification accuracy by producing more robust utterance-level representations.
